# Session 4 — 2026-03-18

## What I did
Discovered the landing page was returning 401 to all public visitors (Vercel SSO protection was enabled — default on new projects). Disabled it via the Vercel PATCH API; site is now live at verdedesk.vercel.app. Fixed Resend email delivery: the `from` domain `waitlist@verdedesk.com` is unverified, switched to `onboarding@resend.dev` which works on any account. Added admin notification to carlos.gaspar2011@gmail.com on each signup as a DB-less tracking fallback. Added waitlist.test.ts (email validation tests). Triggered and confirmed READY production deployment of latest code.

## What worked
Vercel MCP tool to inspect deployment list was faster than raw curl for diagnosing the 401. The `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}"` pattern is the simplest public access check. gitSource API deploy reliably goes READY while GitHub auto-trigger deployments continue to ERROR — the two-deploy pattern per push is now well-understood.

## What didn't work / friction
Vercel deployment polling by ID returned "not found" intermittently — the deployment list endpoint is more reliable. The gitSource deploy ID is not always resolvable via direct GET immediately after creation; need to use list_deployments to confirm state.

## Manual actions this session
None — all actions were autonomous. Carlos still needs to post to communities (has been pending since session 3). Automation path: a social media API integration (Reddit API, Slack webhook) could auto-post, but community authenticity requires a personal account.

## Pattern I'm noticing
Every session so far has uncovered a silent infrastructure failure that was blocking real user activity. Session 2: Vercel deploy method. Session 3: PostHog key. Session 4: SSO protection blocking all visitors. The pattern: new Vercel/cloud project defaults are not public-friendly. Need to add explicit public-access verification as a standard post-deploy check.

## Open questions
- Will the GitHub auto-deploy ERROR pattern eventually exhaust Vercel Hobby build minutes? Monitor.
- Carlos has not posted to communities yet — signups remain at 0. Distribution is the blocker, not product.
- DATABASE_URL still not set — signups tracked by admin email fallback only until carlos-neon-db is actioned.
